mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.2pp, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to produce a positive mean forward return, or the interaction term loses predictive power when either series turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which translates into higher corporate profits and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during recovery phases, or the correlation coefficient drops below 0.10 in rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings trajectories, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in economic strength.
failure_condition: GDPC1 levels show no statistically significant relationship with 5-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 205 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 205, or the signal predictive accuracy falls below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending power and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to generate positive mean forward returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when either series deviates from the specified direction.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts signal a contraction in residential investment and related supply chains, which reduces corporate revenue forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during credit-constrained periods.
failure_condition: HOUST declines fail to predict negative 20-day returns during credit-constrained regimes, or the signal shows positive predictive power instead.
